title: Bitter Tender Korean Mulukhiyah Stew
description: A refreshing and nourishing Korean stew made with mulukhiyah, perfect for a brunch gathering. This dish combines the bitterness of mulukhiyah with the tenderness of pork and the sweetness of Korean chili flakes.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the sliced pork and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the mulukhiyah leaves, Korean chili flakes, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Pour in 4 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes or until the mulukhiyah is tender.
Stir in the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
